content.problemStatement:
Chef went to the store in order to buy one can of coke. In the store, they offer NNN cans of coke (numbered 111 through NNN). For each valid iii, the current temperature of the iii-th can is CiC_iCi​ and its price is PiP_iPi​.

After buying a can of coke, Chef wants to immediately start walking home; when he arrives, he wants to immediately drink the whole can. It takes Chef MMM minutes to get home from the store. However, Chef does not know the value of MMM yet.

The ambient temperature outside is KKK. When a can of coke is outside, its temperature approaches the ambient temperature. Specifically, if its temperature is ttt at some point in time:


if t>K+1t \gt K+1t>K+1, then one minute later, its temperature will be t−1t-1t−1
if t<K−1t \lt K-1t<K−1, then one minute later, its temperature will be t+1t+1t+1
if K−1≤t≤K+1K-1 \le t \le K+1K−1≤t≤K+1, then one minute later, its temperature will be KKK
When Chef drinks coke from a can, he wants its temperature to be between LLL and RRR (inclusive). For each iii between 111 and QQQ inclusive, find the cheapest can for which this condition is satisfied when M=iM = iM=i or determine that there is no such can.
constraints: the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10610^6106
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
the sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 10,00010,00010,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains five space-separated integers NNN, QQQ, KKK, LLL and RRR.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N), the iii-th of these lines contains two space-separated integers CiC_iCi​ and PiP_iPi​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing QQQ space-seperated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the answer for M=iM = iM=i: the price of the can Chef should buy, or −1-1−1 if it is impossible to buy a can such that Chef's condition is satisfied.
